content: Hey everyone, this Example Gentle Cleanser is a low-foam gel built around a mild amino-acid surfactant, glycerin, and panthenol, so it leans toward gentle cleansing and hydration rather than a deep strip-down. It works especially well if you have normal, dry, combination-dry, or tolerant sensitive skin, particularly if you deal with that tight feeling after washing, seasonal dryness, or mild redness. To use it, lather it in wet hands and massage gently for thirty to sixty seconds, then rinse with lukewarm water, once or twice a day depending on how dry or sensitive your skin is. The one-line summary is that it focuses on a mild amino-acid surfactant so your skin does not feel stripped after rinsing, and it is a great entry-level pick if you have dry or sensitive skin. If you have very sensitive skin, it is a good idea to patch test on a small area first before using it on your whole face.
